title: Stochastic Processes & Path Integrals Shadow Analysis

ito_formula.why: Quadratic variation [B,B]_t = t: exp(log t) correction = EML-2

quadratic_variation.description: E[|B_t - B_s|²] = |t-s|: second moment = power law

brownian.note: Brownian paths are EML-∞ (not differentiable, infinite variation) but shadow=EML-2

feynman_kac.description: u(t,x) = E_x[exp(-∫₀ᵗ V(B_s)ds) f(B_t)]: path integral
feynman_kac.why: exp(-∫V·ds): exponential of integral = EML-2 (paired exp+log structure)

partition_function.description: Z = E[exp(-βH)]: canonical partition function over Brownian bridges
partition_function.why: E[exp(·)]: log-partition function F = -log Z = EML-2

feynman_kac.note: Path integral is EML-∞ (infinite-dimensional measure) but shadow=EML-2

characteristic_function.why: exp(iuX_t): complex exponential in characteristic function = EML-3

levy_exponent.why: e^{iux} in the jump part: irreducible complex exponential = EML-3

comparison_with_brownian.brownian: shadow=EML-2: no complex phase in characteristic function (E[exp(iuB_t)]=exp(-u²t/2))
comparison_with_brownian.note: Brownian = real Gaussian → EML-2; Lévy = jump measure with complex phases → EML-3

loewner_equation.description: ∂_t g_t(z) = 2/(g_t(z) - W_t): driven by Brownian motion W_t = √κ·B_t
loewner_equation.why: Conformal map g_t is complex-valued; 2/(g-W) = complex exponential structure = EML-3

hausdorff_dimension.description: dim(SLE_κ) = 1 + κ/8 for κ ≤ 8: fractal dimension
hausdorff_dimension.why: κ parameterizes oscillation amplitude; fractal = EML-3 complexity

correlation_functions.why: Described by SLE_{3}: complex conformal maps = EML-3

free_fermion_spectrum.description: Z = |det(I - K)|: Fredholm determinant at criticality
free_fermion_spectrum.why: Fredholm det = exp(Tr log(I-K)): involves complex eigenvalues of K = EML-3

kpz_equation.description: ∂_t h = ∂²_x h + (∂_x h)²/2 + ξ: KPZ universality class
kpz_equation.why: KPZ exponents (1/3, 2/3): power laws = EML-2; no complex phase

renormalization.why: Self-similar: exp(α log t) = EML-2; real-valued scaling = EML-2

key_theorem: The Stochastic Shadow Rule (S262): Stochastic EML-∞ objects split by whether their characteristic function is real or complex: REAL characteristic function → EML-2 shadow:   Brownian motion (E[e^{iuB_t}]=e^{-u²t/2}: Gaussian, no oscillation),   Feynman-Kac (E[e^{-βH}]: real exponential, log-partition = EML-2),   KPZ/SPDE (real power-law scaling). COMPLEX characteristic function → EML-3 shadow:   Lévy processes (E[e^{iuX_t}]=e^{tψ(u)}: jump measure with e^{iux} term),   SLE (complex Loewner equation), Ising criticality (SLE-described). THE RULE: shadow=3 iff the canonical approximation uses e^{iu·}: complex Fourier/Lévy. shadow=2 iff the canonical approximation uses e^{-real}: Gaussian/heat kernel/partition. This rule is the probabilistic version of the QFT rule (S261): complex phase in the exponential = EML-3; real exponential = EML-2.
rabbit_hole_log: Brownian paths: EML-∞ but shadow=EML-2 (Itô formula, heat kernel — real exp)
Lévy processes: shadow=EML-3 (characteristic function has e^{iux} jump term)
RULE: real exponential kernel → EML-2; complex exponential → EML-3
